name: William Frank Newton
abstract: Frankie Newton (born William Frank Newton; January 4, 1906 – March 11, 1954) was an American jazz trumpeter from Emory, Virginia, United States. He played in several New York City bands in the 1920s and 1930s, including those led by Lloyd Scott, Charlie "Fess" Johnson, Chick Webb, Benny Carter, Sam Wooding, and Lucky Millinder.

about_quotes.speaker: George Wein
about_quotes.evidence: he was more or less of a mentor to me because he indoctrinated me with a taste in music... But he taught me the subtleties of jazz.
about_quotes.doc_title: George Wein — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Flip Phillips
about_quotes.evidence: I played with Frankie Newton in 1939... We opened Kelly’s Stables in New York.
about_quotes.doc_title: Flip Phillips interviewed by Monk Rowe, Sarasota, Florida, April 11, 1996

about_quotes.speaker: Hentoff Nat
about_quotes.evidence: The same thing happened with Frankie. Once I talked to him about music... It was just a natural evolution. Just like I feel privileged to know Clark Terry... We talk about life.
about_quotes.doc_title: Hentoff Nat — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Art Hodes
about_quotes.evidence: I hired Eddie Conlon, Mezzrow, Cliff Jackson, Frankie Newton and Davy Tough at seven dollars apiece, a college date, and I got ten for being leader and getting them together.

about_quotes.speaker: Al Hall
about_quotes.evidence: Frankie Newton was the leader, and Sammy Benskon was the pianist and me who worked there for a while.

about_quotes.speaker: Patricia Willard
about_quotes.evidence: I played with Frankie Newton and his band. We played all over in...in the country over there
